title: cerastium caespitosum subsp. fontanum
intro: Cerastium caespitosum subsp. fontanum is a subspecies of the rock jasmine plant, known for its delicate white flowers and ability to thrive in harsh, rocky environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Cerastium caespitosum subsp. fontanum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
